name: Common Cold (Rhinovirus)
diet: Hydration with warm broths, herbal teas with honey, and high Vitamin C fruits like oranges and kiwis.

name: Influenza (Flu)
diet: Electrolyte drinks, chicken soup, and soft foods like bananas or toast to manage nausea.

name: COVID-19 (Coronavirus)
diet: High-protein foods (eggs, lean meat) for recovery and Vitamin D-fortified foods like mushrooms and fatty fish.

name: Dengue Fever
diet: Papaya leaf juice, coconut water for hydration, and iron-rich foods like spinach to support blood health.

name: Chickenpox (Varicella)
diet: Soft, bland diet (mashed potatoes, yogurt) if mouth sores are present; avoid salty or acidic foods.

name: Measles
diet: Vitamin A-rich foods (carrots, sweet potatoes) to prevent complications and plenty of water.

name: Mumps
diet: Soft foods that require minimal chewing (soups, puddings) due to swollen jaw glands; avoid acidic fruit juices.

name: Hepatitis A, B, & C
diet: High-calorie, low-fat diet to ease liver digestion; strictly avoid alcohol and processed sugars.

name: HIV/AIDS
diet: High-protein and high-calorie nutrient-dense foods; ensure strict food safety/hygiene to avoid infections.

name: Herpes Simplex (HSV-1 & HSV-2)
diet: Foods high in Lysine (fish, chicken, legumes) and low in Arginine (nuts, chocolate) to potentially reduce outbreaks.

name: Epstein-Barr Virus (Mononucleosis)
diet: Anti-inflammatory foods and heavy hydration; avoid alcohol to protect the liver and spleen.

name: Zika Virus
diet: General supportive hydration and a balanced Mediterranean diet to support the immune system.

name: Rabies
diet: Intravenous fluids and nutrition are typically required due to difficulty swallowing (hydrophobia).

name: Yellow Fever
diet: High-carb, low-protein diet during acute phases to prevent liver and kidney strain.

name: West Nile Virus
diet: Immune-boosting diet rich in antioxidants (berries, leafy greens) and adequate fluids.

name: Polio (Poliomyelitis)
diet: High-fiber foods to prevent constipation caused by reduced mobility and muscle weakness.

name: Rubella (German Measles)
diet: Supportive hydration and vitamin-rich light meals.

name: Rotavirus & Norovirus
diet: The 'BRAT' diet (Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast) and oral rehydration salts (ORS).

name: Hand, Foot, and Mouth Disease
diet: Cold foods like yogurt or smoothies to soothe mouth sores; avoid spicy or salty foods.

name: Shingles (Herpes Zoster)
diet: Vitamin B12 and orange/yellow vegetables (Vitamin A) to support nerve health and skin recovery.

name: Human Papillomavirus (HPV)
diet: Folate-rich foods (asparagus, chickpeas) and cruciferous vegetables like broccoli to support cellular health.

name: Strep Throat
diet: Soothing warm liquids, mashed vegetables, and frozen fruit pops to numb throat pain.

name: Tuberculosis (TB)
diet: High-protein (beans, meat), high-calorie diet, and Vitamin B6-rich foods like bananas.

name: Pneumonia (Bacterial)
diet: Warm fluids to thin mucus and small, nutrient-dense meals to avoid shortness of breath while eating.

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
diet: Unsweetened cranberry juice, high water intake, and probiotics (kefir) to maintain healthy bacteria.

name: Salmonella & E. coli
diet: Clear liquids initially, followed by bland, low-fiber foods until digestion normalizes.

name: Lyme Disease
diet: Anti-inflammatory, grain-free or low-sugar diet to manage joint pain and support antibiotic treatment.

name: Meningitis (Bacterial)
diet: High-protein shakes and easily digestible nutrition to support brain and body recovery.

name: Tetanus
diet: High-calorie intake is often necessary due to high muscle metabolic activity; often requires liquid nutrition.

name: Whooping Cough (Pertussis)
diet: Small, frequent meals to prevent vomiting after coughing fits; stay hydrated.

name: Botulism
diet: Medical nutritional support (tube feeding) is usually required due to paralysis of swallowing muscles.

name: Anthrax
diet: High-protein and fluid-heavy diet to support recovery from systemic infection.

name: Cholera
diet: Intense rehydration with Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) and zinc supplementation.

name: Typhoid Fever
diet: Low-fiber, high-calorie diet (boiled rice, baked potatoes) to avoid irritating the intestines.

name: Syphilis
diet: General balanced nutrition to support the immune system during antibiotic treatment.

name: Athlete's Foot & Ringworm
diet: Reduce sugar and yeast intake to limit fungal growth; include garlic for natural antifungal properties.

name: Candidiasis (Yeast Infection)
diet: Low-sugar, low-carb diet; include probiotics (yogurt) and caprylic acid-rich coconut oil.

name: Malaria
diet: Iron-rich foods to combat anemia and high-calorie foods to replace energy lost to high fevers.

name: Giardiasis
diet: Avoid dairy (lactose) temporarily as the parasite can cause temporary lactose intolerance.

name: Toxoplasmosis
diet: Supportive diet rich in Vitamin C and E; strict avoidance of raw or undercooked meats.

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
diet: Omega-3 fatty acids (fatty fish) and anti-inflammatory spices like turmeric and ginger.

name: Lupus (SLE)
diet: Low-sodium diet to protect kidneys; avoid alfalfa sprouts which may trigger flares.

name: Type 1 & Type 2 Diabetes
diet: Low-glycemic index carbohydrates, high fiber (whole grains), and lean proteins.

name: Multiple Sclerosis (MS)
diet: Vitamin D-rich diet, lean proteins, and high-fiber foods to support neurological health and digestion.

name: Celiac Disease
diet: Strict, lifelong gluten-free diet (no wheat, barley, or rye).

name: Down Syndrome
diet: Weight-managed, high-fiber diet to prevent obesity and constipation; focus on heart-healthy fats.

name: Cystic Fibrosis
diet: High-calorie, high-salt, and high-fat diet with fat-soluble vitamin (A, D, E, K) supplements.

name: Sickle Cell Disease
diet: Folate-rich foods (lentils, leafy greens) and heavy hydration to prevent pain crises.

name: Hemophilia
diet: Iron-rich foods and Vitamin K-rich greens to support blood health; maintain a healthy weight to reduce joint stress.

name: Huntington's Disease
diet: High-calorie, easy-to-swallow foods to counteract involuntary movements and prevent choking.

name: Hypothyroidism
diet: Iodized salt, selenium-rich Brazil nuts, and limit raw goitrogenic foods like kale.

name: Hyperthyroidism
diet: Avoid excess iodine; include cruciferous vegetables which may naturally slow thyroid hormone production.

name: Gout
diet: Avoid purine-rich foods (red meat, organ meats, beer) and increase water and cherry intake.

name: Phenylketonuria (PKU)
diet: Very low-protein diet; avoid high-protein foods like meat, dairy, and aspartame (Equal).

name: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
diet: The DASH diet: Low sodium, high potassium, magnesium, and calcium.

name: Coronary Artery Disease & Heart Failure
diet: Low-saturated fat, low-sodium, and high-omega-3 diet (Mediterranean style).

name: Stroke
diet: Heart-healthy diet with focus on reduced salt and soft-textured foods if swallowing is impaired.

name: Asthma & COPD
diet: Lower carb/higher fat (for COPD), magnesium-rich foods to relax airways, and avoid sulfites.

name: Sleep Apnea
diet: Weight-loss focused diet to reduce neck fat; avoid alcohol and heavy meals before bed.

name: Alzheimer's & Parkinson's
diet: MIND or Mediterranean diet rich in antioxidants, berries, and healthy fats (olive oil).

name: Epilepsy
diet: Standard balanced diet; some patients use a medically supervised Ketogenic (high-fat) diet.

name: Migraine
diet: Avoid triggers like aged cheese, MSG, nitrates (deli meats), and red wine; maintain steady blood sugar.

name: GERD (Acid Reflux)
diet: Avoid spicy foods, chocolate, caffeine, and mint; eat small meals and stay upright after eating.

name: IBS & IBD (Crohn's/Colitis)
diet: Low-FODMAP diet for IBS; low-fiber/bland diet during IBD flares; high-protein for healing.

name: PCOS
diet: Low-glycemic, anti-inflammatory diet to manage insulin resistance; high-fiber and lean proteins.

name: Addison's Disease
diet: High-sodium diet (especially in hot weather or during exercise) and adequate complex carbs.

name: Acromegaly
diet: Diabetes-friendly diet to manage potential growth-hormone-induced insulin resistance.

name: Lung/Breast/Prostate Cancer
diet: Plant-based, high-fiber diet; include cruciferous vegetables and green tea; maintain protein during treatment.

name: Major Depressive/Anxiety Disorder
diet: Omega-3s, fermented foods for gut health, and B-vitamins to support neurotransmitter function.

name: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome
diet: Vitamin C and Copper-rich foods to support collagen synthesis; high-protein to support joint stability.

name: Myasthenia Gravis
diet: Soft, easy-to-chew foods to prevent fatigue while eating; small, frequent meals.
